member.name: T:LJCNetCommon5.LJC
member.summary: Contains common static methods.

remarks.para: The LJCNetCommon class contains static methods for all .NET program types.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJC.CheckArgument``1(``0)
member.summary: Check for missing argument of type: string with no value, null, integer = 0, IList with no items, decimal = 0 or DataTable with no rows.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJC.CompareNull(System.Object,System.Object)
member.summary: Compare null values.
member.returns: The integer that indicates the order relationship of the objects.
member.remarks:
-2 - Neither object is null.
-1 - Null object x precedes object y.
0 - Object x and object y are both null.
1 - Object x follows null object y.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJC.ConfigBool(System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves the Config bool value.

remarks.para: This uses the program config file which is the name of the program plus the extension ".config".

// An example config file.
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<configuration>
<appSettings>
<add key="LocalDbDataAccess" value="true" />
</appSettings>
</configuration>

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJC.ConfigColor(System.String,System.Drawing.Color@)
member.summary: Retrieves the Config Color value.

remarks.para: This uses the program config file which is the name of the program plus the extension ".config".

// An example config file.
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<configuration>
<appSettings>
<add key="BeginColor" value="AliceBlue" />
</appSettings>
</configuration>

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJC.ConfigString(System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves the Config string value.

member.name: T:LJCNetCommon5.LJCAssemblyReflect
member.summary: Provides Assembly Reflection methods.

remarks.para: Provides information for Constructors, Types, Methods, Properties and Fields.
The object reflection properties are set using the methods: SetAssembly(), SetConstructorInfo(), SetFieldInfo(), SetMethodInfo(), SetPropertyInfo() and SetTypeReference().
Once the reflection properties are set, the Syntax strings can be retrieved with the syntax methods: Get*Syntax();

member.name: T:LJCNetCommon5.LJCDataColumn
member.summary: Represents a Data Column definition.

remarks.para: The LJCDataColumn object is used extensively throughout the LJC libraries, utilities and applications; wherever data elements need to be dynamically defined or manipulated. It is commonly used to define database table columns, grid control columns and text file columns and is often used to facilitate moving data between a database table column, grid control column, text file column or other data source and a data object.

A Data Object is an instance of a class that contains properties for each data element in a data source row. It is a strongly typed, object oriented representation of the data source row for use in code. It may contain common methods to assist with cloning, sorting and displaying an object string identifier.

When the ColumnName property is set, it will set the PropertyName property to the same value if PropertyName is null. This is the most common scenario where the data object property name is the same as the data source column name.

If a data object property name is different than the data source column name, then the PropertyName value must be set to the data object property name. For join columns, it may be the case that a different PropertyName needs to be chosen as there is already a property with that name. This often means that a RenameAs value must be assigned as there is most likely also a table column name conflict with the primary table.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCDataColumn.FormatValue
member.summary: Formats the column value for the SQL string. (D)

remarks.para: Returns the string formatted value from the Value property based on the DataTypeName property.

If the DataType is Boolean, returns "1" or "0".
If the DataType is DateTime, returns a string Date or Minimum Date value.
If the DataType is string, returns a string with doubled single quotes.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCDataColumn.op_Implicit(LJCNetCommon5.LJCDataColumn)~LJCNetCommon5.LJCDataValue
member.summary: Creates a DbValue object from a LJCDataColumn object. (E)

member.name: P:LJCNetCommon5.LJCDataColumn.ColumnName
member.summary: Gets or sets the ColumnName value. (R)
member.remarks:
This is the Table Column name for the SQL statement. The result value is retrieved using this name. The Table Name comes from DBMessage.DbQuery.TableName. 
      select
        TableName.ColumnName
        
      update TableName set
        TableName.ColumnName = value

member.name: P:LJCNetCommon5.LJCDataColumn.Position
member.summary: Gets or sets the Position value. (R)
member.remarks: This property is useful for data sources that require a column position value. An example would be a text file that does not contain column headings.

member.name: P:LJCNetCommon5.LJCDataColumn.PropertyName
member.summary: Gets or sets the PropertyName value. (R)
member.remarks: This is the Data Object Property name. The result value is mapped into the Property with this name.

member.name: P:LJCNetCommon5.LJCDataColumn.RenameAs
member.summary: Gets or sets the RenameAs value.
member.remarks:
This result name is usually the same as the Table Column name. The RenameAs value changes the result name. The result value is retrieved using this name. The Table Name comes from DBMessage.DbQuery.TableName. 
      select
        TableName.ColumnName as RenameAs

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCHTMLBuilder.AddChildIndent(System.String,LJCNetCommon5.LJCTextState)
member.summary: Adds the new (child) indents.
member.remarks:
Adds an indent for following child elements.
The indent will only be added if the createText length > 0 and the textState.ChildIndentCount > 0.

member.summary: Changes the IndentCount by the provided value.
member.param: The amount to increment or decrement the indent count.

member.summary: Indicates if the builder text ends with a newline.
member.returns: true if builder text ends with a newline; otherwise false.

member.summary: Indicates if the builder has text.
member.returns: true if builder has text; otherwise false.

member.summary: Gets the current indent length.

member.summary: Allow text to start with a newline.
member.param: Indicates if a new line can be added.
member.returns: true if allowed to start with a newline; otherwise false.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCHTMLBuilder.AddLine(System.String)
member.summary: Adds a text line without modification.
member.remarks: AddLine() is an "Append" method. It adds the unmodified text line to the builder.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCHTMLBuilder.AddText(System.String)
member.summary: Adds text without modification.
member.remarks: AddText() is an "Append" method. It adds the unmodified text to the builder.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCHTMLBuilder.Line(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Adds a modified text line to the builder.
member.param: Indicates if the element can be indented.
Indicates if a new line can be added.
member.returns: The potentially indented and wrapped new text value.
member.remarks:
Line() is an "Append" method. It creates the text with GetText() and adds a new line after the text. The text is then added to the builder.
Line() works the same as Text() except it appends a new line.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCHTMLBuilder.Text(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Adds modified text to the builder.
member.param: Indicates if the element can be indented.
Indicates if a new line can be added.
member.returns: The potentially indented and wrapped new text value.
member.remarks:
Text() is an "Append" method. It creates the text with GetText() and adds it to the builder.
Note: One should set the "addIndent" parameter to false when adding text to the builder after getting the text with a "GetText" method. This is because the the indent has already been added with the "GetText" method.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCHTMLBuilder.GetAttribs(LJCNetCommon5.LJCAttributes,LJCNetCommon5.LJCTextState)
member.summary: Gets the attributes text.
member.remarks:
The returned text does not end with a new line. This allows for additions after the text.
Begin, Create and End text will start with a new line if the builder text already has a value.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCHTMLBuilder.GetIndented(System.String)
member.summary: Gets a new potentially indented line.
member.returns: The potentially indented new text value.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCHTMLBuilder.GetIndentString
member.summary: Returns the current indent string.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCHTMLBuilder.GetLine(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Gets a modified text line.
member.param: Indicates if the element can be indented.
Indicates if a new line can be added.
member.returns: The potentially indented and wrapped new text value.
member.remarks:
GetLine() is a "GetString" method. It creates and returns the potentially indented and wrapped text. It will start the returned text with a new line if the builder already has text.
It appends a new line to the returned text. The applied indent is the product of the class properties IndentCount and IndentCharCount. (IndentCount * IndentCharCount).
The "addIndent" parameter defaults to true. Set it to false to prevent indenting the line further.
The "allowNewLine" parameter defaults to true. Set it to false to prevent starting with a new line.
Wraps the text with a newline if the WrapEnabled parameter is true and the line length is greater than LineLimit.
GetLine() works the same as GetText() except it appends a new line.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCHTMLBuilder.GetText(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Gets potentially indented and wrapped text.
member.param: Indicates if the element can be indented.
Indicates if a new line can be added.
member.returns: The potentially indented and wrapped new text value.
member.remarks:
GetText() is a "GetString" method. It creates and returns the potentially indented and wrapped text. It will start the returned text with a new line if the builder already has text.
The applied indent is the product of the class properties IndentCount and IndentCharCount. (IndentCount * IndentCharCount).
The "addIndent" parameter defaults to true. Set it to false to prevent indenting the line further.
The "allowNewLine" parameter defaults to true. Set it to false to prevent starting with a new line.
Wraps the text with a newline if the WrapEnabled parameter is true and the line length is greater than LineLimit.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCHTMLBuilder.GetWrapped(System.String)
member.summary: Gets added text and new wrapped line if combined line > LineLimit.
member.returns: The potentially wrapped and indented new text value.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCHTMLBuilder.Begin(System.String,LJCNetCommon5.LJCTextState,LJCNetCommon5.LJCAttributes,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Appends the element begin tag.
member.param: Indicates if the element can be indented.
Indicates if the child elements should be indented.
member.remarks:
Begin() is an "Append" method. It creates the element opening tag with GetCreate() and adds it to the builder. The appended text does not end with a new line. This allows for additions after the text.
The added text will start with a new line if the builder text already has a value.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCHTMLBuilder.Create(System.String,System.String,LJCNetCommon5.LJCTextState,LJCNetCommon5.LJCAttributes,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Appends an element.
member.param: Indicates if the element can be indented.
Indicates if the child elements should be indented.
Indicates if the element will be closed.
member.remarks:
Create() is an "Append" method. It creates the element text with GetCreate() and adds it to the builder. The appended text does not end with a new line. This allows for additions after the text.
The added text will start with a new line if the builder text already has a value.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCHTMLBuilder.End(System.String,LJCNetCommon5.LJCTextState,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Appends the element end tag.
member.param: Indicates if the element can be indented.
member.remarks:
End() is an "Append" method. It creates the element text with GetEnd() and adds it to the builder.
The appended text does not end with a new line. This allows for additions after the text.
The added text will start with a new line if the builder text already has a value.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCHTMLBuilder.GetBegin(System.String,LJCNetCommon5.LJCTextState,LJCNetCommon5.LJCAttributes,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Gets the element begin tag.
member.param: Indicates if the element can be indented.
Indicates if the child indent should be applied.
member.remarks:
GetBegin() is an "GetText" method which creates the element opening tag with GetCreate() and returns the text value.
Note: One should set the "addIndent" parameter to false when adding text to the builder after getting the text with a "GetText" method. This is because the the indent has already been added with the "GetText" method.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCHTMLBuilder.GetBeginSelector(System.String,LJCNetCommon5.LJCTextState)
member.summary: Gets beginning of style selector.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCHTMLBuilder.GetCreate(System.String,System.String,LJCNetCommon5.LJCTextState,LJCNetCommon5.LJCAttributes,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Gets an element.
member.param: Indicates if the element can be indented.
Indicates if the child indent should be applied.
Indicates if the element will be closed.
member.remarks:
GetCreate() is a "GetText" method which creates the element or element opening tag and returns the text value. It is called from other element create methods.
The addIndent parameter defaults to true, which starts the text with the current indent string.
The isEmpty element defaults to false, which does not add the empty element slash.
If isEmpty is true, the empty element slash is added and the close parameter is ignored. The element will not include the closing tag.
The ChildIndentCount is incremented if the element is not empty and not closed.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCHTMLBuilder.GetEnd(System.String,LJCNetCommon5.LJCTextState,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Gets the element end tag.
member.param: Indicates if the element can be indented.
member.remarks: GetEnd() is a "GetText" method which creates the element closing tag and returns the text value.

member.summary: Creates a US Census Soundex value.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCNetString.IsSoundexLetter(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Checks if the letter is a soundex skipped letter.
member.returns: true if the letter should be skipped; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: True if the letter is the first letter or not a vowel and not the same as the previous letter.

member.name: M:LJCNetCommon5.LJCNetString.Phonetic(System.String,System.Int32@,System.Nullable{System.Char}@)
member.summary: Creates a Phonetic character from the supplied text starting at the supplied index.
member.returns: true if a Phonetic was created; otherwise, false.

remarks.para:
Sets the letter parameter to:
- "F" if the current and following letter are "PH"
- "S" if the current letter is "P", the following letter is "S" and the third letter is a vowel or "Y".
- "K" if the current letter is "C" and the following letter is "A", "O" or "U".
- "S" if the current letter is "C" and the following letter is "E", "I" or "Y".
- Otherwise, the same letter as at the current index.
Advances index to the next available char.

member.name: T:LJCNetCommon5.LJCReflect
member.summary: Provides object property reflection capabilities.

remarks.para: This class is commonly used when accessing properties of a Data Object. The object reference is passed to the constructor.
The property objects can then be accessed with: GetPropertyNames(), GetPropertyType(), GetValue(), SetPropertyValue() and SetValue()
The typed property values can be retrieved with: GetDateTime(), GetDbDateString(), GetInt32() and GetString().
